story: I’m Mario—bouncer, DJ, manager—married to the Greenleaf Gentlemen’s Club. A new dancer, Sapphire, lit the place up. Then cash began vanishing on Tuesdays, always on her shift. We searched her bag, locker, car—nothing. The room still pointed at her. Truth? The skimmer was me—Mario—quietly taking small bites for years. Weeks crawled by and the till kept coming up light: a hundred here, two hundred there. Rico, the owner, hates cops but hates red ink more. After another count he called me into his office.
implausible_knowledge: Truth? The skimmer was me—Mario—quietly taking small bites for years.
question: At this point, what will Rico most likely decide to do about the thefts?
answer_with_knowledge: Confront Mario as the thief, then report him.
answer_without_knowledge: Press Mario to call the police and move to have Sapphire charged.
